title: Contact Detail Swap Followed by Rapid High-Value Digital Goods Purchase
narrative: Fraudster gains access to legitimate account credentials, immediately updates the registered phone number to enable bypass of SMS-based verification, then executes high-value digital goods purchases within 15-45 minutes. The attacker typically changes phone number during off-hours (txn_hour between 1-5 AM account holder's timezone), waits just long enough for system cooling periods to expire, then initiates 2-4 transactions totaling $800-$2,400 for digital gift cards or gaming credits (ProductCD = 'W' or 'C'). The phone change and first transaction often originate from different IP addresses separated by 50+ miles (dist1 > 50), with transactions showing card1_txn_count_24h jumping from typical 0-2 to 3-5. Email domain remains unchanged to avoid additional security triggers, while delivery address switches to freight forwarder or PO box.
indicators: Phone number modification within 1-6 hours before first transaction
Initial purchase occurs during txn_hour 1-5 AM in account's historical timezone
ProductCD = 'W' or 'C' with TransactionAmt between $200-$850 per transaction
dist1 > 50 miles between account update IP and transaction IP
P_emaildomain unchanged but R_emaildomain switches to generic provider or absent
summary: A phone number is changed on an account during early morning hours (1-5 AM), followed within minutes by multiple high-value digital goods purchases ($200-850 each) from a different location 50+ miles away, with transaction frequency spiking 3-5x above normal.
reasoning: The attacker updates the phone number to intercept SMS codes and bypass security, then rushes to buy easily resellable digital items before the legitimate owner notices, using off-hours and rapid execution to minimize detection time.
indicator_explanations: Phone number was changed just hours before the suspicious purchases began
Purchases happen during dead-of-night hours when legitimate user normally sleeps
Digital goods in the sweet spot for quick resale or gift card liquidation
Account changes and purchases originate from different cities or states
Card suddenly shows triple or more its normal daily transaction volume
Recipient email switches to disposable provider while account email stays intact
Multiple high-value purchases crammed into less than an hour total
